title: Stable learning establishes some common ground between causal inference and machine learning
methods: paradigm comparison (IID vs. transfer vs. stable learning)

data_scope.primary_dataset: none — no new empirical dataset introduced
data_scope.motivating_examples: COMPAS (US criminal justice), image classification (dogs/grass), ICU treatment, recommendation systems
data_scope.algorithmic_results: theoretical bounds O(delta/lambda) proven; empirical results in referenced prior works (KDD 2018, AAAI 2020, arXiv 2021)
data_scope.literature_coverage: 1983-2022, spanning statistics, econometrics, and machine learning

core_conclusion: Spurious correlation — from confounding, data selection bias, or non-causal associations — is the root cause of instability, poor explainability, and unfairness in ML models. Stable learning uses sample reweighting to decorrelate input features (global balancing), identifying direct causal predictors and achieving robust prediction across unknown distribution shifts without requiring full causal identification assumptions. This establishes genuine common ground between causal inference and machine learning, with implications for explainability and fairness.
why_it_matters: Reframes ML-causality as mutually beneficial for prediction, not just intervention; identifies spurious correlation as a unified root cause of stability, explainability and fairness failures; loosens strict causal assumptions while retaining causal benefits; provides plug-in algorithms (SRDO, deep global balancing) for high-stakes deployment.
why_nature: Cross-disciplinary synthesis at the paradigm level (statistics/econometrics meets CS/ML); addresses a fundamental limitation of the learning paradigm rather than an incremental fix; high societal urgency around AI fairness and reliability; authored by prominent cross-domain figures (Cui/Tsinghua + Athey/Stanford).
added_value_to_my_research: Spatial decorrelation extension of global balancing to remove geographic confounders in R2SFCA accessibility models
Spatially-indexed group fairness via stable learning sample reweighting for health geography disparity analysis
SRDO as pre-decorrelation step before MGWR/GWR bandwidth selection to separate causal spatial non-stationarity from collinearity artifacts
Causal structure pre-filter for multi-source spatiotemporal data fusion (mobile phone + remote sensing + POIs) to isolate true health predictors
Global balancing loss embedded as graph-level decorrelation constraint in physics-informed GNN for stable cross-city transfer

related.title: A causal perspective on dataset bias in machine learning for medical imaging
related.relation: applies causal reasoning to address dataset bias and fairness in ML for medical imaging, directly extending stable learning's fairness and spurious-correlation themes

related.title: Algorithmic fairness and bias mitigation for clinical machine learning with deep reinforcement learning
related.relation: addresses fairness and bias mitigation in clinical ML, complementing stable learning's fairness implications with a reinforcement learning approach

related.title: Improving generalization of machine learning-identified biomarkers using causal modelling with examples from immune receptor diagnostics
related.relation: uses causal modelling to improve ML generalization across datasets, directly applying the stable-learning principle of causal features for robust prediction

related.title: Deep learning of causal structures in high dimensions under data limitations
related.relation: automated causal structure discovery that could serve as a pre-filter for stable learning's global balancing, enabling fully data-driven causal-stable pipelines

related.title: Causal inference and counterfactual prediction in machine learning for actionable healthcare
related.relation: concurrent perspective on bringing causal inference into ML for healthcare prediction, complementary to stable learning's framework

related.title: Reliable deep learning in anomalous diffusion against out-of-distribution dynamics
related.relation: addresses out-of-distribution reliability in deep learning, empirically validating the kind of distribution-shift robustness stable learning theoretically targets

figures.caption: Three ways of generating correlations: causal (a), confounding (b), and selection bias (c), illustrating which produce spurious vs. stable associations with outcome Y
Physical data-generating processes over time for image classification and recommendation systems, motivating the causal structure perspective on prediction problems
Comparison of IID learning, transfer learning, domain generalization, and stable learning positioned by objectives with respect to testing distributions
